top_priority.summary: Address 22 high-risk bug source(s), starting with security.SRIGenerator.Generate
top_priority.why: 22 functions have structural risk scores above 0.50. These are structurally predisposed to defects: they have high fan-in (changes break many callers), sit in mixed-role clusters (responsibility confusion), or are in junk-drawer files (merge conflicts). Splitting and relocating them reduces defect risk.
steps: Start with security.SRIGenerator.Generate (risk 0.64) — mixed-role cluster (responsibility confusion), 43 LOC (3x median), junk-drawer file (multi-concern).
Extract distinct concerns into separate functions with clear input/output contracts.
Add targeted tests for functions with high fan-in — a bug there affects many callers.
Move functions out of junk-drawer files into files matching their cluster's role.
Repeat for the remaining 21 high-risk functions.
